all_steps.name: Outside lands+bootcamp
all_steps.description:
Just slept at my place for the first night!!
Upon arrival in SF Jon picked me up, and barely having slept the night before I crashed at 9P.M and woke up at 8:30A.M skipping jetlag! The day after I met Kiva and we went to dinner with some of his friends (at Gavin Newsom's place :0). On Friday we visited Vasco at Founders Inc. Lab in Fort Mason (he is on the startup grind) which was super sick (met the automated chessboard dude and Ukrainian drone guy) and we found me an Outside Lands ticket!!

The first weekend was awesome, kicking of my US journey with a festival at Golden Gate park and an amazing group of people :)) as well as an amazing line up! (still woozy, Tyler the creator, Thundercat, Doechii!!, Sammy Virji, Royel Otis, Bonobo, Anderson Paak, ...). Traditionally, we ended the weekend at the palace of Fine arts (thanks to Tessa's brother picking us up <3).

It was a quick switch into school mode getting up at 7A.M to go to orientation (which was a bit lame but had free food + met some people). I left a bit early to go bouldering with Kiva, and had dinner with the Corshen's after. The rest of the week flew by, as I drove to class and back every day and explored, did my 'homework' and fixed some paperwork.

Yesterday I took the Ferry from North Bay to SF (transit for free with my student card hehe) and hung out with Helena all day before officially moving into my place and trying to make it cozy already.

all_steps.name: Back in Fairfax with mam&pap
all_steps.description:
Mam en pap op bezoek!!

We hung out in SF (went to the deYoung museum in Golden Gate park which was cool), Berkeley (4th street) and Fairfax, and our prime destination was IKEA!! 

We got a bunch of stuff for the apartment and it's starting to feel more like home :)

Burning man madness!!

Decides to send it on Friday, and after prepping for two days, waiting out a storm and 7 hours in line we got into Burning Man. The most positively overwhelming random place on Earth.

Biking around in the desert with giant art pieces and an infinite amount of music, while making spontaneous pitstops to DJ on the church, at a trampoline park, a hot air balloon, a roller skate rink, a wrestle dome, a skatepark, a fire spitting octopus, a huge library??,  dance parties with free drinks, a BBQ at 5A.M and moreeee. 

It was sick and I couldn't have wished for a better first week of uni hehe :)

Emmaaaaaaaa

Left early on Friday to arrive around noon! We visited campus, went to the park and then made burritos for dinner. :))
The next day we slept in too late (classic), got fish and chips and went for a hike with a little cold plunge at the end (I forgot my swimming shorts whoops) (my actual shorts got wet because of a wave double woops). At night we went to see the gang at a weird Oktoberfest bar, it was super nice being back together! 

Sunday it was time for a visit to Vancouver, we hung out around Canada place + Granville island, then went home for an afternoon of games and making pizza.
Monday I woke up at 4:45, boarded my flight on last call at 7, to be in lecture at 11 and on campus till 21 woopwoop

all_steps.name: New York+Baltimore
all_steps.description:
Week in New York! 
Started out a bit rough... 
But ended up hanging out with Helena and friends, Nara and friends, Charlotte and friends, dad & Ariel, saw Danyal again after 2 years ...  Anyway, a lot of friends! 
Then Wednesday-Thursday it was time for a little sidequest: observing Dr. Lew Schon's full ankle replacement surgery in Baltimore. We woke up at 5A.M and had a PACKED day, but definitely worth it as I got to talk to everyone in the clinic and learned a lot!

Joshua tree for Kiva's bday!

Reunited with the outside lands gang + many more cool people. We stayed at a huge airbnb and embarked on a heavy (and much needed) party weekend: 15 people simultaneously in a hot tub, an insane live music show from Paul and André, heavy rain and poncho troubles in the National Park and amazing stargazing.

Partying & nature resulted in a weekend without even touching my phone! Heart is full :)

Week in LA for a real American Thanksgiving!

I drove down with Jon & Shana on Tuesday to spend the holiday them & their extended family :) I caught Tessa on Tuesday night before she left for SF and mainly hung out with Aylo, Kiva and their cousins (Zach, Liv & Hannah) for the rest of the week. Besides an awesome Thanksgiving dinner went to the beach (and saw some cringe proposals), played pickleball (hungover lol), did an escape room, went to a Bas & the Hics concert in the Blue Note, hot tubbed a bunch, ate overly expensive Korean bbq, went toprope climbing up a 30m cliff(4)!, and I managed to meet up with Anjali and her boyfriend as well on Saturday. 

I skipped the ride back up on Saturday to have dinner with Sergio and his family, and decided to fly out on Monday instead. The rest of the weekend was mainly studying and guess what... I ended up MISSING my flight because I didn't bring my passport to LA...

Anyway, after some paperwork I managed to rebook for free the next day (without my papers :0), which meant going for drinks again with Tessa & Kiva at Tom Bergen's on Monday night. 

Deadlines are stacking up and the rest of the week will be messy, but this trip was a success!

all_steps.name: End of semester :0
all_steps.description:
A dip of insecurity adapting to the new environment of Berkeley overachievers, a lot of work for Calsol and Borelli's control class, zero work for some other classes and capstone lol, a bike lost on the bus, a ton of basketball, football and volleyball games (including a trip to Stanford to lose the Big Game >:( ), taco tuesdays, I-house happy hours, a crushing finals week with several all nighters and some sidequests later the end of the semester has arrived! 

I probably didn't lock in as hard as I said I would, but oh well there is always next semester ;) 
Although, ski season has arrived ...

all_steps.name: Cervinia
all_steps.description:
Boys skitrip!
Barely landed from the US, we embarked on a 10hr drive to the mountains.
2 days carving away, 2 days on the board failing tamedogs and jumping around as usual, and one crazy powday where we got a guide to do some touring. Dad couldn't keep up, and I took a huge fall because my ski got stuck in a treacherous branch (and dissapeared for 20 mins, see pic 10), but it was an AWESOME day.

2 weekjes terug thuis!
Dirk, Louise en Alexis zijn nog eens langsgekomen in Antwerpen en er werden veel avonturen beleefd.
Van Nieuwjaar bij Mika met iedereen (hottubben, dansen en dineren) tot de UB snowboarden en ID Kaffee open jam in Delft. 
Ontelbaar veel cafe avondjes (elke dag gedronken letterlijk wtf), extravagante familiebrunches en ons jaarlijkse restaurantuitje met 4, een marathon supporterclub vormen voor Wannes op Oudjaar, random Duitsers door het raam bij de Muze ontmoeten, Ethan van SF spontaan mee naar Zeezicht nemen, bootcamp zoomlessen om 5u 's ochtends en de gewoonlijke gekke gesprekken in huize Rubin5Coin. 

Altijd fijn on terug te zijn :))

all_steps.name: Biggie reunion
all_steps.description:
9 days back at Big White!

One big snow day and a whole lot of melting the rest of the week, the snow was bad but the company was great :)

My skibag was missing upon arrival and I had to rent for two days (classic). But I still got a pretty good deal as I crashed on Joe's air mattress at the gang's flat and used Leah's skipass for free!

4 nights out at Sams (with US frats visiting), the roadtrip aftermovie premiere, boardgames and quizzes, an intense WHL hockey game in Kelowna and a not so intense staff hockey game against ski patrol, a railjam in Telus park, bullwheel lunches and bingo at HV. I jumped right back into old habits!

Until next time biggie :)

all_steps.name: Tahoe weekend 1
all_steps.description:
We booked an Airbnb in Tahoe for the weekend!

Me and Taner raced to the slopes to get the last hour on Friday afternoon, Saturday had shit conditions and I was mainly teaching Logan and Karisma, and Sunday was suuuuper fun. 

I got sick over the weekend (and accidentally claimed the yoghurt for myself whoops), but watching x games and ski videos combined with sauna sessions made it better. We met Christians from Texas in the hottub and "argued" over who would pay the tip when we split a table with random people.

I dropped my stuff at Alex's place, so I can go back anytime without having to rent a huge car!

all_steps.name: Tahoe weekend 2: skiclub edition
all_steps.description: Enough snow to burry a house, too much dumb stuff (dirty girlscout shots, failed shuttle adventures, disgusting espresso martinis with Stanford peeps found on the lift) and slight logistical challenges later I would still call it a successful weekend.

all_steps.name: Tahoe 3 - midweek boys trip
all_steps.description:
Late night ride up to Tahoe with Taner and his undergrad friends (Lucas and Zacky) on Wednesday (after coming back Sunday lol) we got to stay at Alex's for two days (so much easier then in Truckee or an Airbnb wow). 

 After two days of slushy spring snow and parklaps (hit crazy air on the quarterpipe, decent tube 50-50s and working on a CRAZY new trick (bs cork 540 handdrag? bs Miller 5? I don't even know what it is). I am more sore than I have been in a long time after snowboarding.

We hit the classic in n out sesh on the way back and visited UC Davis campus and Taner's old house to get back to Berkeley just in time (kind of) for barcrawl.

all_steps.name: New Orleans for AAOS
all_steps.description:
Saw Dr. Schon again at the conference! (And he even went to dinner with the peeps in Belgium The following week haha). Sanjana joined last minute and I forced Taner to come by buying his ticket, so it was full house. 

The conference hall at AAOS was super cool,  I was networking HARDDD, got free headshots, and played around with some cool tech. Not sure how useful it was tho, I did kind of land a job at Shukla (definitely thanks to last minute shopping for a blazer), but I'm not going to Florida lol. 

Other than that NOLA wasn't very welcoming. The city is kind of disgusting and trashy, and not in the elegant jazzy way (why do they like Daiquiris and neon so much?), though the old buildings are nice. We ended up hanging out at the Airbnb playing loads of games and watching music videos, or around Tulane university (in this hang spot called the rat) and Audubon Park all the time.

We got plastered at the Boot & the Palm, had great food (especially from this place called hot stuff, the clams are awesome but the beignets are overrated though), we weren't allowed to climb trees and skipped too many breakfasts. 

All in all, not a bad time.

all_steps.name: Tourist in Berkeley
all_steps.description:
Gang visits Berkeley + SF.

Quick recap: taking over the whole appartment to sleep, chick fil a, good breakfasts (Taner would be proud), White party, campus day (they were impressed by frat row & we got into the campanile for free), Edmonds DANCE PARTEYYY (Fantine cookeddd with the music, we met some weird spiritual guy on the stairs & I got glade girls number).
Huge ass walk from oakland (homeless man threw a shoe at the girls :0), ferry to downtown (starred in a movie?!?), then walked to at&t + chase center, then mission, then waymo to Haight Ashbury (bought pants, not juggling balls), then GGP with live band, on to baker beach for sunset and fisherman's wharf for overpriced mid Italian food and sea lions. To then end the night getting rejected from monroes (but the girl's and my pants got Darius in!)

all_steps.name: Santa Cruz & Big Sur
all_steps.description:
On to the Roadtrip!

Konden geen camping vinden dus hebben last minute gebeld naar David Morales en daar een plekje gefikst. Tijdens bellen, fout afgeslagen en op een coole spot geraakt. Laurane heeft gekronkeld op gekke wegen in het donker on aan te komen bij de verwelkomende Morales fam. Daar hebben we lekker eten gekregen (beste Mango Lassi ooit en breakfast specials), zijn we door hun voorraad wijn heengegaan rond her kampvuur en hebben we uiteindelijk 2 nachten geslapen.  

Aaron en fam hebben ons over dag door Monterrey en Big Sur gegids en we hebben in n out gegeten! We zijn lange Pebble Beach gereden, hebben een kampvuur op het waaierige strand gemaakt en Wannes heeft een nachtzwenmetje gedaan.

all_steps.name: Highway 1 down to Cambria
all_steps.description:
Drove highway one further down through Big Sur for a couple of days. AMAZING
 Stopped in jullia pfeiffer for swim and walk and lunch, then whatever cove but it was closed,  as well for climb and hang (with beer) on the beach. Then we got to campsite after sunset made fire and Zaira visited the first night, while Aaron passed by the second night. We passed by the Elephant seal vista point, sand dollar beach, and Laurane settled down as we finally booked stuff for sequoia and yosemite.

all_steps.name: Sequoia
all_steps.description: Onze camping ligt aan een meer en Wannes en ik zijn meteen een loopje gaan doen. Veel gitaar en codenames gespeeld, en onze kapotte batterij en foutje kabel geregeld. We kwamen in Sequoia en er was een sketchy weg die afgeraden werd maar eig super chill was maar de wandelepot toe. Daar beklonmen we 2000 jaar oude bomen en hadden Wannes en ik een leuke fotografie shoot. Savonds rustig weer een kampvuurtje, met spelletjes zoals white box spelen tot we uitgepur in slaap vielen.

all_steps.name: Yosemite National Park
all_steps.description:
2 days Yosemite!

No hot showers at redneck John's farm, but we slept on a nice campsite between the cows. Our weirdly calculated groceries left us with hotdogs, noodles and 50 leftover eggs for dinner and we had a great time hanging around the campfire again. 
We did two big hikes, I Trailran Upper Falls down in 50min the first day (I was so dead) and the next day the girls lapped us down from Stanford point (beautiful view of the valley, Half Dome and El Cap, + way less busy!) to prove they were faster than we are. Loser had to do the dishes (there was a heated discussion about the definition of being the first IN the camper).

The last night I had to get us stuck in the middle of the highway whoops, luckily someone towed us back into drive able circumstances (don't tell our parents) and we brought back Maggie alive and we'll.  Eind goed, al goed (although we left our strawberries at El Monte!)

all_steps.name: Yosemite half Marathon
all_steps.description:
Taner convinced me to buy tickets for the half marathon like two weeks ago, sooo we barely trained but it was good. 

Ran a decent time but took too many breaks to stretch ankles, eat orange slices and go to the bathroom lol. Zaki placed 16th overall and 3rd in our age group tho wtf?

We camped out near Bass Lake (so much space we had a storage tent), almost had a bear alarm, had a lot of guitar time while cooking and juggled a lot (learned around the world :0 ). Now let's lock in for examsss.

all_steps.name: Datedinner & finals week
all_steps.description:
Klaar met examens donderdag (amper gestudeerd pfff, motivatie was ver te zoeken). Leuk afgesloten met een date dinner dezelfde avond! Was super cute en we hadden allemaal cadeautjes (pet voor Sanjana's bday, cake voor Alex's bday, t shirts voor onze fake startup). 

De volgende dag een kleine Mitsubishi gehuurd en naar lake Anza gegaan. Grad weekend lekker druk, vrijdag klokslag 22u campanile dijkel, waar alle graduates bij de toren samenkwamen om een dijkel te doen en te feesten. Direct daarna Charlotte ophalen van het vliegveld om dan om 2u30 aan te komen in napa.

all_steps.name: Napa
all_steps.description:
Eerste dag lekker geslapen en gechilld aan het zwembad, om dan te dorphoppen en lekker eten op te zoeken (weliswaar niet altijd gelukt). In Petaluma was een vintage car show (new dream car: Karmann Ghia) en zijn we na een tijdje proberen ontsnappen aan het geluid door eentje te gaan drinken, maar tevergeefs aan gezien de bar overvloed was door babies.

De volgende dag was het tijd voor een wijntour! In een 13de eeuws kasteel (waarvan de stenen uit Europa zijn geshipped, super dom) hebben we 10+ wijnen geproefd onder leiding van onze Italiaanse gids (heel straf accent en hij zei 107 keer "guys-a" tijdens de tour). Al bij al werkte hun business plan wel, want we zijn vertrokken met een aantal wijnflessen. 

Maandag vroeg op om terug richting Berkeley te gaan!

all_steps.name: Grad!
all_steps.description:
We got the weird cap and gown and went around campus with the gang for a graduation photoshoot!

The fam is visiting and we went to a rooftop bar with everyone (20 people!) after the (somewhat boring) grad ceremony in the Greek Theatre. The student speech was very good, but the guest speaker (some Intel executive) was mid.  A huge bill and an inaugural, intergenerational dijkel later (ondanks ik men ID niet meehad geregeld) we headed off to the next bar! (accidentally almost in the wrong car whoopsies)

all_steps.name: Yosemite with fam
all_steps.description:
Evergreen lodge
4 beren gezien?
Trouw op glacier point
Veel stenen beklimmen
Heel de tijd verloren gelopen en niet genoeg voorbereid (geen kaarten gedownload, geen wandelingen voorbereid en eenbeetje traag op gang elke ochtend) maar veel plezier

Mirror lake + glacier point
Hetch hetchy reservoir, chillings onder de brug.
